(Brazilian Portuguese: [[ˈbo(w)ɐ ˈvistɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"), literally *Good View*; figuratively "Fairview") is the capital of the Brazilian state of [Roraima](/wiki/Roraima "Roraima"). Situated on the western bank of the [Branco River](/wiki/Branco_River "Branco River"), the city lies 109 km from Brazil's border with [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela"), and 58 km away from the border with [Guyana](/wiki/Guyana "Guyana"). It is the only [Brazilian state capital](/wiki/List_of_capitals_of_subdivisions_of_Brazil "List of capitals of subdivisions of Brazil") located entirely north of the equator.

**Boa Vista** (Brazilian Portuguese: [[ˈbo(w)ɐ ˈvistɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"), literally *Good View*; figuratively "Fairview") is the capital of the Brazilian state of [Roraima](/wiki/Roraima "Roraima"). Situated on the western bank of the [Branco River](/wiki/Branco_River "Branco River"), the city lies 109 km from Brazil's border with [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela"), and 58 km away from the border with [Guyana](/wiki/Guyana "Guyana"). It is the only [Brazilian state capital](/wiki/List_of_capitals_of_subdivisions_of_Brazil "List of capitals of subdivisions of Brazil") located entirely north of the equator.

Boa Vista is the most populous municipality in the state of Roraima;[[3]](#cite_note-3) approximately half of the population of the state lives in the city. Commerce mostly occurs with [Manaus](/wiki/Manaus "Manaus"), the capital of the [State of Amazonas](/wiki/Amazonas_(Brazilian_state) "Amazonas (Brazilian state)"). Business also takes place between Boa Vista and the cities of [Lethem](/wiki/Lethem,_Guyana "Lethem, Guyana"), in Guyana and [Santa Elena de Uairén](/wiki/Santa_Elena_de_Uair%C3%A9n "Santa Elena de Uairén"), in Venezuela. Boa Vista also has a cultural and commercial brotherhood relationship with the city of [Caruaru](/wiki/Caruaru "Caruaru"). These three foreign cities are the only major cities that can be accessed from Boa Vista by road, although roads connect other smaller state municipalities with the capital city. Travel by airplane is the only means of transportation with other regions of the country.

As a modern city, Boa Vista stands out among the other capitals of the [North Region](/wiki/Northern_Brazil "Northern Brazil") of Brazil as it is a [planned city](/wiki/Planned_city "Planned city") with a [radial plan](/wiki/Radial_plan "Radial plan"). It was planned by the architect Darci Aleixo Derenusson who based his design for the city on one that is similar to that of Paris, France. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The city was built under the direction of Captain Ene Garcez, the first governor of Roraima.

Boa Vista, 1975. [National Archives of Brazil.](/wiki/Brazilian_National_Archives "Brazilian National Archives")

The municipality of Boa Vista formed the first urban area of the state of Roraima. The São Joaquim do Rio Branco Fort, founded in 1775, is located on the banks of the [Uraricoera River](/wiki/Uraricoera_River "Uraricoera River") about 32 km from the capital and is considered to be of great importance to the region.

The city was created on July 9, 1890, as Boa Vista do Rio Branco. It was founded by [Augusto Villeroy](/wiki/Augusto_Ximeno_de_Villeroy "Augusto Ximeno de Villeroy") (Amazonas's Governor). The first mayor was [João Capistrano da Silva Mota](/w/index.php?title=Jo%C3%A3o_Capistrano_da_Silva_Mota&action=edit&redlink=1 "João Capistrano da Silva Mota (page does not exist)"), also known as Coronel Mota. After the mayor, two councillors ([José Francisco Coelho](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Francisco_Coelho&action=edit&redlink=1 "José Francisco Coelho (page does not exist)") and [José Gonzaga de Souza Junior](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Gonzaga_de_Souza_Jr.&action=edit&redlink=1 "José Gonzaga de Souza Jr. (page does not exist)")) were appointed.

In 1943, during the middle of World War II, Boa Vista became the capital of the recently created Federal Territory of [Rio Branco](/wiki/Roraima "Roraima"). The territory grew from mining operations in the area. The then Federal Territory of Rio Branco was elevated to statehood, later being renamed as "[Roraima](/wiki/Roraima "Roraima")". Later, machine-based mining was prohibited (because of the resulting damage to the landscape), which ended up hindering the economy of the [state](/wiki/States_of_Brazil "States of Brazil") and the municipality.

In the 2010s, the [crisis in Venezuela](/wiki/Crisis_in_Venezuela_(2012%E2%80%93present) "Crisis in Venezuela (2012–present)") led to the population swelling by around 50,000 Venezuelan immigrants.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Tourism and recreation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Tourism and recreation")]

* Anaua Park, a major recreational park is located in Boa Vista. It is the biggest park in the [North Region of Brazil](/wiki/North_Region,_Brazil "North Region, Brazil").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] It has a Forrodromo (show place), a square, a lake and a kart-racing facility.
* Taumanam is a small artificial beach on the Rio Branco. It has several stores and snack bars, and was created in 2005.
* The [Ayrton Senna](/wiki/Ayrton_Senna "Ayrton Senna") complex is the most visited tourist attraction in Boa Vista.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] It consists of Ayrton Senna, Velia Coutinho (Alimentation Square and a show place), Praça das Águas (a square with a water show), Millennium Portal (it was created to commemorate the 21st century), and Praça das Artes (the newest and the largest plazas in the complex).

### Festa Junina (Saint John Festival)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Festa Junina (Saint John Festival)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Orla_de_Boa_Vista_5.JPG/250px-Orla_de_Boa_Vista_5.JPG)](/wiki/File:Orla_de_Boa_Vista_5.JPG)

Orla Taumanan Park

[Festa Junina](/wiki/Festa_Junina "Festa Junina") was introduced to Northeastern Brazil by the [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_people "Portuguese people") for whom St John's day (also celebrated as [Midsummer](/wiki/Midsummer "Midsummer") Day in European countries), on 24 June, is one of the oldest and most popular celebrations of the year. The festivities traditionally begin after 12 June, on the eve of St Anthony's day, and last until the 29th, which is Saint Peter's day. During these fifteen days, there are bonfires, fireworks, and folk dancing in the streets (step names are in French, which shows the mutual influences between court life and peasant culture in the 17th, 18th, and 19th-century Europe). Once exclusively a rural celebration, today, in Brazil, it is now largely a city festival.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### The Civic Center

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: The Civic Center")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4a/Garimpeiro.JPG/250px-Garimpeiro.JPG)](/wiki/File:Garimpeiro.JPG)

Praça dos Garimpeiros-Miners' monument.

The Civic Center, built in the form of an opened fan, starts from the banks of the Rio Branco. This design was created by the engineer [Darci Aleixo Deregusson](/w/index.php?title=Darci_Aleixo_Deregusson&action=edit&redlink=1 "Darci Aleixo Deregusson (page does not exist)") during Ene Garcez's government, the first of its kind in Roraima.

From the Civic Center runs a system of 16 avenues, with inspiration drawn from Paris, France, [Belo Horizonte](/wiki/Belo_Horizonte "Belo Horizonte"), and [Goiânia](/wiki/Goi%C3%A2nia "Goiânia"), Brazil. In this square are the head offices of the Executive, Judicial and Legislative branches of the state government. Other buildings of significance are the Palace of Culture, the Amazônia Bank, the Ministry of Education, and a cathedral.

The civic center also has a monument to miners.

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Boa Vista had significant relative growth in the 1970s and 1980s, especially due to its placement as the state of [Roraima](/wiki/Roraima "Roraima")'s capital, with the urban population of the city growing exponentially from 1970 to 1980 relative to the outer rings of countryside,[[7]](#cite_note-geo-7) with it more than quadrupling since 1980.

According to the 2022 Census by [IBGE](/wiki/IBGE "IBGE"), the city is predominantly [pardo](/wiki/Pardo "Pardo"), with them making up 62.2% of the population.[[8]](#cite_note-ibgb-8)

The city's most important stadium is [Estádio Flamarion Vasconcelos](/wiki/Est%C3%A1dio_Flamarion_Vasconcelos "Estádio Flamarion Vasconcelos"), better known as *Canarinho*, located in the neighborhood of the same name, Canarinho. Also located in Boa Vista is the [Estádio Raimundo Ribeiro de Souza](/wiki/Est%C3%A1dio_Raimundo_Ribeiro_de_Souza "Estádio Raimundo Ribeiro de Souza"), better known as the *Ribeirão*, which is located in a suburb of the city.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Geography")]

The city is situated on the western bank of the Branco River, and it lies 220 km from Brazil's border with Venezuela. It is the only Brazilian state capital located entirely north of the equator.

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Climate")]

Boa Vista experiences a [tropical savanna climate](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") ([Koppen](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *Aw*), with a hot, humid wet season and a very warm dry season. Being the only major Brazilian city north of the equator, the wettest and driest months in Boa Vista are the reverse of the rest of the Amazonian region of Brazil, with a hot, humid, rainy period from April to November and a very warm, dry period from December to March. Temperature fluctuations are relatively small throughout the year. When considering the four seasons (spring, summer, fall, winter), summer (June to August) is the rainiest and coldest season, while fall (September to November) is the hottest season.

The [Maracá Ecological Station](/wiki/Marac%C3%A1_Ecological_Station "Maracá Ecological Station") was established by presidential decree on 2 June 1981. The station consists of the island of Maracá between the Santa Rosa and Maracá branches of the [Uraricoera River](/wiki/Uraricoera_River "Uraricoera River") in the municipality of Boa Vista, and has an area of 101,312 hectares (250,350 acres).[[9]](#cite_note-9)
The strictly protected area was established with the purpose of preserving a representative sample of the Amazon ecosystem.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### International Airport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: International Airport")]

Boa Vista is served by [Boa Vista-Atlas Brasil Cantanhede International Airport](/wiki/Boa_Vista_International_Airport "Boa Vista International Airport") with direct flights to [Brasília](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia "Brasília"), [Georgetown](/wiki/Georgetown,_Guyana "Georgetown, Guyana") (Guyana), and [Manaus](/wiki/Manaus "Manaus"). Boa Vista International Airport was opened on February 19, 1973, and underwent major remodelings in 1998 and 2009. The passenger terminal, runway and apron were all enlarged, and a separate taxiway was built.

[Boa Vista Air Force Base - ALA7](/wiki/Boa_Vista_Air_Force_Base "Boa Vista Air Force Base"), one of the most important bases of the [Brazilian Air Force](/wiki/Brazilian_Air_Force "Brazilian Air Force"), is located in Boa Vista.

### Highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Boa_Vista,_Roraima&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Highways")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b9/BR-174_Sul.jpg/250px-BR-174_Sul.jpg)](/wiki/File:BR-174_Sul.jpg)

BR-174, Boa Vista/Venezuela to Manaus.

The federal government has started a project to link Boa Vista by road to Manaus and then by extension to the major centers of Brazil, by asphalting the [BR-174](/wiki/BR-174 "BR-174") and building several bridges in the Amazon forest, allowing a direct road link to Manaus and then on over the [BR-319](/wiki/BR-319 "BR-319") to the central regions of Brazil.[[22]](#cite_note-22)
